zh.Syleham, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可希勒哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Jane Decker於1453年出生在英國素福爾可希勒哈姆時，她的父親Nelson Decker30歲，母親Hannah Harris28歲。她於1470年與Edward Watson Clark在英國素福爾可希勒哈姆結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1470年之後。

zh-hans.Syleham, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可希勒哈姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Jane Decker于1453年出生在英国素福爾可希勒哈姆时，她的父亲Nelson Decker30岁，母亲Hannah Harris28岁。她于1470年与Edward Watson Clark在英国素福爾可希勒哈姆结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1470年之后。

nameMeanings.name: Decker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: occupational name for a roofer (thatcher, tiler, slater, or shingler) or a carpenter or builder, from an agent derivative of Middle High German decke ‘covering’, a word which was normally used to refer to roofs, but sometimes also to other sorts of covering; modern German Decke still has the twin senses ‘ceiling’ and ‘blanket’.
Dutch: variant of   Dekker  , cognate with 1 above. Compare   De Decker  .
English (London): variant of   Dicker  .

variants.name: Dekker
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Gerrit, Maurits, Egbert, Gerben, Hendrik, Henk, Martinus, Wim.
Dutch: occupational name for a roofer (a thatcher, slater, shingler, or tiler), Middle Dutch deck(e)re, an agent derivative of decken ‘to cover’.
English: variant of   Dicker  .

variants.name: De Decker
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Willem.
Dutch: variant of Decker , with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’.

variants.name: Deckman
meanings: Americanized form of German Deckmann: variant of Decker 1.

variants.name: Deka
meanings: Polish: nickname or occupational name from Old Polish deka ‘pillow’ (from Middle High German decke) or from a word meaning ‘dagger, dirk’. Alternatively, it may be an adaptation of German Decker or Deck .
Indian (Assam): from the Ahom military rank deka ‘four thousand’, referring to the four thousand troops commanded by such an officer. Compare Barua .

variants.name: Deckert
meanings: German:
variant of   Decker   1.
from an old personal name, Tagahard, composed of Old High German tac ‘day’ + hart ‘strong’.

variants.name: Deck
meanings: South German: metonymic occupational name for a roofer, from Middle High German decke ‘covering, roof’. Compare the later form Decker .
Dutch and North German: from the personal name Deck, a local pet form of Dutch Diederik (see Dederick ) or German Dietrich .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Jane Decker was born in 1453, in Syleham, Suffolk, England, her father, Nelson Decker, was 30 and her mother, Hannah Harris, was 28. She married Edward Watson Clark in 1470, in Syleham, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died after 1470.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sarah Jane Decker est née en 1453, à Syleham, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Nelson Decker, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Hannah Harris, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Edward Watson Clark en 1470, à Syleham, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1470.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sarah Jane Decker 1453 in Syleham, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nelson Decker 30 und ihre Mutter Hannah Harris 28. Sie hat 1470 in Syleham, Suffolk, England Edward Watson Clark geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1470.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Jane Decker è nata il nel 1453 nel Syleham, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Nelson Decker, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Hannah Harris, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Edward Watson Clark nel 1470, nel Syleham, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1470.

ja.Syleham, Suffolk, England: イギリススフォルクシレハム
ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Deckerが1453年にイギリススフォルクシレハムで生まれた時，父Nelson Deckerは30歳，母Hannah Harrisは28歳であった。1470年にイギリススフォルクシレハムでEdward Watson Clarkと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1470年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Decker(이)가 1453년 영국 수폴크 실함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nelson Decker(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Hannah Harris(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1470년 영국 수폴크 실함에서 Edward Watson Clark(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1470년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Jane Decker nasceu em 1453, em Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nelson Decker, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Hannah Harris, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Edward Watson Clark em 1470, em Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 1470.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Jane Decker родилась в 1453 г. в местности силэам, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Nelson Decker было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Hannah Harris, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edward Watson Clark в 1470 г. в местности силэам, суффолк, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1470 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sarah Jane Decker nació en 1453, en Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Nelson Decker, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Hannah Harris, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Edward Watson Clark en 1470, en Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió después de 1470.
